title: TTT Verification Note v1.3 — Golden-Ratio Solids as the Asymmetric Self-Referential Solution of the Hyperbolic-Zero Equation
description: Machine verification (Python/sympy, exact symbolic arithmetic) showing that the golden ratio φ arises as the unique asymmetric self-referential solution of the Hyperbolic-Zero equation Y=1/X (symmetric condition Y=X gives X=±1; minimal asymmetric condition Y=X+1 gives X=1/φ, exactly). This is connected to the previously machine-verified φ-bipartition of the five Platonic solids (icosahedron and dodecahedron embed φ exactly in their coordinates; tetrahedron, octahedron, and cube do not). The squared circumradius-to-inradius ratio is shown to share a common factor of 3 across all solid families: 3² (tetrahedron), 3&sup9; (octahedron/cube), and 3(7-4φ) (icosahedron/dodecahedron). The product of the φ-group enclosing-sphere volume ratios (2.037 × 2.749 = 5.599) is compared to the observed dark-matter-to-baryonic-matter ratio (~5.4), differing by 3.7%. A working hypothesis [C] is presented connecting the algebraic property that asymmetric solutions of Y=1/X cannot be defined without a paired counterpart to the observational property that dark matter has never been detected in isolation. All physical identifications are explicitly classified as interpretive [B]/[C]; only the algebraic and geometric identities are classified [A]. Supplement to TTT v2.0 (10.5281/zenodo.19704117) and successor to TTT_Verification_Note_v1.1/v1.2.

creators.name: Kawakami, Naoyuki
creators.affiliation: Team Shiojiri / Cooperate Craft Wine Makers

notes: Reproduction: pip install sympy; python3 verify_golden_ratio_v13.py — all assertions pass, exit code 0. Code license: MIT. This note is exploratory; physical identifications of golden-ratio solids with dark matter are working hypotheses, not established results.
